§6Gasoline§f

§aA high-energy fuel from naphtha — burn it in generators, toss it as a fire bomb, or lead it for even more punch!§f

Gasoline is a combustible, high-grade fuel produced from §bNaphtha§f in the §bChemical Plant§f. With §e605 kTU§f and §e1.51 MHE§f per bucket, it makes an excellent generator fuel. It can also be leaded for higher performance at the cost of heavy metal pollution.

§6Production§f:
§b• Chemical Plant§f: §e1,000mB§f Naphtha → §e800mB§f Gasoline (requires §bGasoline Production§f template).

§6Uses§f:
§b• Leaded Gasoline§f (Chemical Plant): §e10,000mB§f Gasoline + Tetraethyllead → §e12,000mB§f Leaded Gasoline (§e907 kTU§f / §e2.26 MHE§f per bucket).
§b• Leaded Gasoline§f (Industrial Mixer): Same recipe, different machine.
§b• Napalm B§f: Gasoline + Slimeball in §bBlast Furnace§f → napalm.
§b• Tossable Gas Can§f: Craft any gasoline container → throwable fire bomb.
§b• Chainsaw§f: Usable as fuel (up to §e5,000mB§f).

§eTip§f: §bLeaded Gasoline§f provides §e50%§f more energy per bucket but adds §bheavy metal§f pollution, making nearby glyphids stronger. Use regular gasoline for daily operations and save leaded fuel for emergencies!
